feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: SpaceX plans record stock market debut that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire
entry.content: SpaceX says it plans to raise up to $75 billion when it goes public this month in what could be the largest stock market debut ever, and it would put Elon Musk on course to becoming the first trillionaire.

entry.title: Victim of "horrifying" Chelsea murder identified after 26 years
entry.content: "Chelsea Jane Doe," who was found brutally murdered in Massachusetts, has been identified as Tiffany Bradley of Pennsylvania.

entry.title: Some students with disabilities rely on screens at school. What happens if they're banned?
entry.content: Some students with disabilities rely on assistive technology to learn, and they worry it could be swept up in the movement to get screens out of schools.

entry.title: With World Cup set to begin, public health officials hope to defend against germs
entry.content: The World Cup gets underway next week and millions will pack huge stadiums to watch. Public health officials will be watching too, only they're looking for germs that could spread at these matches!

entry.title: Weakened public health powers could hamper states' outbreak responses
entry.content: Public health experts are worried that states are less prepared to respond to the Ebola outbreak and other infectious disease threats because many weakened their public health authorities in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.

entry.title: A lawsuit challenges Hawaii homestead leases limited to those with 50% Hawaiian blood
entry.content: A lawsuit is challenging the Hawaiian Homes Commission Act, which provides land to Native Hawaiians with at least 50% Hawaiian blood quantum

entry.title: Sherpa guide missing for a week on Mount Everest rescued while crawling to base camp
entry.content: Sherpa guide missing for a week on Mount Everest rescued while crawling to base camp

entry.title: Karen Bass to advance in LA mayor's race as Pratt and Raman vie for final spot
entry.content: CBS News projects that incumbent Mayor Karen Bass will advance to the November election, while her opponents, Councilmember Nithya Raman and political newcomer Spencer Pratt, compete for the final spot.

entry.title: The Knicks’ Jalen Brunson can already do what the Spurs are still figuring out
entry.content: When the New York Knicks needed a big basket against the San Antonio Spurs in Game 1 of the NBA Finals, they turned to Jalen Brunson. He delivered.

entry.title: With Trump in a holding pattern on Iran war, allies and critics worry he risks getting boxed in
entry.content: With Trump in a holding pattern on Iran war, allies and critics worry he risks getting boxed in

entry.title: 3 shot at high school graduation outside Sacramento, officials say
entry.content: Three people were shot at a high school graduation ceremony near Sacramento, officials said.

entry.title: Benchmark raises its first-ever growth fund as part of $2B capital raise
entry.content: The legendary abandons its more than 20 year tradition of keeping its funds to about $425 million.

entry.title: Trump to nominate Todd Blanche for permanent attorney general
entry.content: President Donald Trump nominated his acting attorney general, Todd Blanche, to be the country’s permanent top prosecutor TKTK, Trump said TKTK ADD TRUMP / WH ANNOUNCEMENT TKTK Blanche took over as acting attorney general in early April after Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi, whose deputy Blanche had been.

entry.title: Trump expected to nominate Todd Blanche as permanent attorney general
entry.content: President Trump is expected to nominate Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to serve in the role permanently, several sources familiar with the matter told CBS News.

entry.title: The Hormuz Squeeze Is Redrawing the Oil Map for Good
entry.content: Saudi Arabia, the U.A.E. and Iraq are pouring money into pipelines, rail and storage to keep oil flowing even when the Strait of Hormuz isn’t.

entry.title: Ohio State reaches $100 million settlement over school doctor accused of sex abuse
entry.content: The school has fought lawsuits in federal court since 2018 brought by former student athletes against the university over its failure to stop abuse by Dr. Richard Strauss.

entry.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to renew ceasefire if Hezbollah cuts off attacks
entry.content: The Israeli and Lebanese governments have agreed to implement a ceasefire, after weeks of deadly fighting between Israel and Hezbollah had imperiled broader negotiations between the U.S. and Iran to end their conflict.

entry.title: SPLC asks judge to weigh sanctions against DOJ for sending out unsigned indictment copy
entry.content: The Southern Poverty Law Center asked a judge to consider sanctioning federal prosecutors, after the DOJ shared an unsigned and unstamped copy of a superseding indictment with members of the media.

entry.title: Possible flesh-eating screwworm case in Texas, USDA says
entry.content: A possible case of the flesh-eating New World screwworm is being investigated in Texas, the USDA reported Wednesday.

entry.title: Flesh-eating New World screwworm found in Texas calf, USDA says
entry.content: The USDA said the only animal affected was a 3-week-old calf in Zavala County, Texas, after larvae were identified in its umbilical area.

entry.title: A Short Seller’s Fraud Conviction Is Spooking Wall Street
entry.content: Traders who bet on stock-price declines worry that prosecutors are equating their tactics with market manipulation.

entry.title: Russia’s Elite Is Souring on the War. Putin Doesn’t Seem to Care.
entry.content: Some of Russia’s best known hawks have become more open in expressing a belief that Moscow simply doesn’t have the capacity to achieve an outright victory against Ukraine.

entry.title: Iran attacks Kuwait airport as Israel, Lebanon reach tentative ceasefire
entry.content: Israel and Lebanon agreed Wednesday to renew their fragile ceasefire and create a number of "pilot" security zones inside Lebanon where Hezbollah would be banned.

entry.title: Trump Tells Aides He Won’t Resume All-Out War With Iran Unless U.S. Troops Are Killed
entry.content: Repeated strikes by Tehran have ratcheted up pressure on the president and cast doubt over the ceasefire’s long-term viability, officials said.

entry.title: Why New York Knicks’ Mike Brown didn’t ask for ‘advice’ from Gregg Popovich ahead of NBA Finals
entry.content: New York Knicks head coach Mike Brown knows Gregg Popovich too well to ask the San Antonio Spurs legend for NBA Finals advice. Brown’s answer carried humor, history, and a clear understanding of the matchup in front of him.

entry.title: Adam Silver anticipates 2027 launch for NBA Europe, with a 16-team league and limited promotion system
entry.content: The NBA is coming to Europe. We're slowly learning how that will work.

entry.title: UFC Vegas 118’s Brendan Allen not feeling love for fighting unranked contender – ‘I’m not a likable guy’
entry.content: No love. Top Ultimate Fighting Championship (UFC) Middleweight contender Brendan Allen makes his first appearance of the year when he takes on unranked knockout artist Edmen Shahbazyan in the co-main event of UFC Vegas 118 this weekend (Sat., June 6, 2026) inside the Meta Apex in Las Vegas, Nevada. Allen being matched up with Shahbazyan […]

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama turns heads with pregame outfit for Game 1
entry.content: Victor Wembanyama’s arrival for Game 1 of the NBA Finals on Wednesday garnered a lot of attention online. Fans talked about his outfit, as well as his demeanor as he made his way to the San Antonio Spurs’ locker room.…

entry.title: Bayern Munich star Lennart Karl sets milestone in Germany’s 4-0 win over Finland
entry.content: The FC Bayern star continues to deliver even at international level

entry.title: New intel chief is a partisan warrior who has the president’s ear, sources say
entry.content: Former officers worry Bill Pulte could use intelligence to target political opponents or justify federal oversight of elections.

entry.title: ICE protesters who interrupted church service won't face state charges: Prosecutor
entry.content: Dozens of anti-immigration enforcement protesters who face federal criminal charges after they interrupted a Minnesota church service in January, accompanied by former CNN journalist Don Lemon, will not additionally face state charges

entry.title: Quick commerce FirstClub doubles valuation to $255M in nine months
entry.content: The Bengaluru startup has crossed 1 million orders and reached a $50 million annualized GMV run rate within a year of launch.

entry.title: How AI is upending the fashion industry... and how students are adapting
entry.content: New York City has long been considered the fashion capital of the world, but fashion jobs have declined 30% over the past decade. Evyn Moon visited New York's Fashion Institute of Technology to find out how future generations are reinventing the industry in the AI era.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
If you want the rainbow, you have to tolerate the rain.
Lucky Numbers: 19, 68, 23, 55, 33, 69

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Rub a walnut on damaged wooden furniture to cover up scratches.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: The first toilet ever seen on television was on "Leave It to Beaver."

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Ralph Marston
entry.content: “Expect the best of yourself, and then do what is necessary to make it a reality.” — Ralph Marston

entry.title: Hostage situation at bank building in California ends after suspect shot dead
entry.content: The suspect was pronounced dead at the scene early Wednesday morning, the Bakersfield Police Department said.

entry.title: House votes to rein in Trump on Iran as war loses GOP support
entry.content: In May, the Senate advanced a similar measure to force the president to end the prolonged conflict with Iran.

entry.title: CBS News fires longtime 60 Minutes correspondent Scott Pelley
entry.content: CBS News has fired longtime 60 Minutes correspondent and former "CBS Evening News" anchor Scott Pelley one day after he had a tense and confrontational exchange with new 60 Minutes executive producer Nick Bilton.

entry.title: Terms Revealed for SpaceX’s Unconventional $75 Billion IPO
entry.content: The company plans to sell shares at $135 apiece, eschewing the norm of setting a price range and incorporating investor feedback.

entry.title: Silicon Valley Stalwart Benchmark Breaks From Past, Embraces Mature Startups
entry.content: After a late-stage bet on Cerebras delivered big returns, Benchmark decided to raise its first-ever growth fund.

entry.title: The Crypto Winter Turns Colder as Bitcoin Extends Its Slide
entry.content: The digital token fell for a fourth straight day after a sale by one of bitcoin’s biggest cheerleaders.

entry.title: Energy, water use and pollution of AI and data centers rival most countries
entry.content: The environmental footprint of data centers already rivals some of the world's largest countries, according to a United Nations University report, which also predicts their water and energy use and pollution will double in just four years as use of artificial intelligence grows.

entry.title: Radio scans find no alien tech from the latest interstellar comet
entry.content: The group leading the charge in the search for extraterrestrial life has given the all clear: An interstellar comet looks to be completely natural and free of any alien tech.

entry.title: California tech CEO arrested after feds say he supplied sanctioned computer equipment to Iran
entry.content: A California tech CEO is facing 20 years in prison after allegedly earning millions by selling U.S.-origin computer networking equipment to Iran's military and nuclear establishments, Justice Department officials said.

entry.title: Cannons lost underwater during American Revolution going on display at Georgia museum
entry.content: Georgia's oldest city is welcoming a truckload of historical treasures from the earlier period of U.S. history

entry.title: Shia LaBeouf pleads guilty to simple battery for punching bargoers during Mardi Gras
entry.content: Actor Shia LaBeouf has pleaded guilty to three counts of simple battery for punching people outside a New Orleans bar in February during Mardi Gras

entry.title: Poland and Lithuania confirm exploring a bigger role in nuclear deterrence
entry.content: Poland and Lithuania confirm they are in talks about their role in NATO's nuclear deterrence efforts, which are centered on U.S. nuclear weapons in Europe

entry.title: What Is Personalized Pricing—and Why Are Lawmakers Scrambling to Ban It?
entry.content: Companies already track your every move online. Some researchers say it is only a matter of time until retailers start using that data to set prices just for you.

entry.title: DRC Ebola outbreak could have begun as early as January, WHO chief says
entry.content: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says the virus ‘had a big head start’ but that the response was catching upThe Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo could have begun as early as January, the head of the World Health Organization said, giving the virus “a big head start”.Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus also said the response was being hindered by blanket travel restrictions and highlighted high levels of community mistrust and low levels of contact tracing as key concerns. Continue reading...

entry.title: Uber capping internal use of AI coding software after blowing through budget
entry.content: Uber is setting limits on how much employees can spend on artificial intelligence agents after its workers blew through their yearly AI coding budget in just four months.

entry.title: Federal Regulators Investigating George Santos Over Suspicious Prediction-Market Trading
entry.content: Prediction market Kalshi referred Santos’s suspected trading in a contract that referenced his own appearance at the State of the Union.

entry.title: Anti-abortion activists are trying to limit access in NSW – and they are just getting started
entry.content: Obstetricians and gynaecologists say anti sex-selective abortions bill ‘predicated on misinformation’ and ‘underlying aim is to restrict access to abortion’Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe man who wants to ban “sex-selective abortions” is the first person who will tell you it won’t work.New South Wales Libertarian party MLC John Ruddick has introduced legislation that would see health practitioners sent to prison or fined thousands if they carry out a termination because of the sex of a foetus. Continue reading...

entry.title: NASA declares its Mars Maven spacecraft dead after six months of silence
entry.content: After six months of radio silence, NASA's Maven spacecraft at Mars has been declared dead.

entry.title: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg oil terminal before it hosts Russian economic forum
entry.content: Ukrainian long-range drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and set it ablaze, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Wednesday, sending smoke billowing over the city where President Vladimir Putin was born as it hosts Russia's leading event for attracting foreign capital.

entry.title: European Union launches tech sovereignty initiative to boost chips, cloud and AI at home
entry.content: European Union leaders, worried about the continent relying too heavily on American companies for AI and cloud computing services and Asia for microchips, are pushing back.

entry.title: DOJ investigating George Santos for insider trading on Kalshi
entry.content: Federal prosecutors are investigating former Rep. George Santos over wagering on his own attendance at President Trump's State of the Union address after the prediction market he placed bets on flagged the case.

entry.title: Standoff with bomb-carrying man enters second day at California bank
entry.content: The FBI said Wednesday it is negotiating with a man holed up inside a Bakersfield, California, bank with a bomb strapped to his body and multiple hostages.

entry.title: MAHA tops MAGA in Iowa's GOP governor's contest
entry.content: Republican businessman Zach Lahn defeated Rep. Randy Feenstra in Iowa's GOP gubernatorial primary Tuesday, dealing a rare primary loss to President Trump after his late endorsement failed to push his chosen candidate over the finish line.

entry.title: The week's bestselling books, June 7
entry.content: The Southern California Independent Bookstore Bestsellers list for Sunday, June 7, 2026, including hardcover and paperback fiction and nonfiction.

entry.title: Artist Wyland suing FIFA, others after his Dallas whale mural is painted over
entry.content: An artist has filed a $25 million federal lawsuit against FIFA over his whale-filled mural in Dallas being painted over ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

entry.title: 'I return stolen motorbikes to their owners'
entry.content: 'I return stolen motorbikes to their owners'

entry.title: NYC's 9/11 memorial fundraises to educate youth with $25M match from Mike Bloomberg
entry.content: The 9/11 Memorial and Museum has launched a $75 million fundraising campaign as the nonprofit tries to educate the millions of U.S. youth who don’t remember the terror attacks

entry.title: Bazoumana Toure: Man United plot to hijack Newcastle’s move
entry.content: Manchester United are ready to hijack Newcastle United’s move for Bazoumana Touré as the Red Devils begin the transfer window at a blistering pace, according to a new report.Midfield RebuildManches...

entry.title: Who is Maja Chwalinska? The Polish qualifier on stunning run to French Open semi-finals
entry.content: 24-year-old Chwalinska is only the second woman to go through qualifying to reach the French Open semi-finals, after Nadia Podoroska in 2020

entry.title: Jakub Mensik reacts after passing Ivan Lendl with historic Roland Garros win over Joao Fonseca
entry.content: Jakub Mensik reached the first Grand Slam semi-final of his career after beating Joao Fonseca at Roland Garros. The 20-year-old also passed Ivan Lendl as the youngest Czech man in the Open Era to reach a Grand Slam singles semi-final.

entry.title: Conte bids farewell to Napoli after ‘two winning years’
entry.content: Antonio Conte has said farewell to Napoli supporters online as he leaves his position as head coach after two years, two trophies and two consecutive top four finishes: ‘Two winning years with lots ...

entry.title: Real Madrid have no plans to re-sign former youth product this summer amid growing interest
entry.content: Real Madrid may have another important transfer decision to make this summer as the future of Victor Munoz continues to attract growing attention across Europe.According to AS, the Spanish giants are ...

entry.title: Every Serie A player called up for the 2026 World Cup
entry.content: Italy have painfully missed out on the World Cup for the third time in a row, yet Serie A fans will still see many of their idols in the USA, Mexico and Canada this summer, with 66 players from Italy�...

entry.title: Fantasy Sleepers, Busts & Bold Predictions: The 2026 Miami Dolphins
entry.content: There are always bad receiver rooms somewhere in the NFL. Who can forget Josh Allen having to throw to Robert Foster, Zay Jones and Kelvin Benjamin as a rookie? This year, though, might be particularly special in that regard. In…

entry.title: MLB outfield power rankings entering week 11
entry.content: The 2026 MLB season is gearing up for week eleven. Through the first ten weeks, numerous players have gotten off to fast starts. There have been front-runners for All-Star selections, early cases for season-long awards, as well as rookies quickly…

entry.title: Big tobacco uses cigarette playbook to help sell ultra-processed foods, journal reveals
entry.content: New issue of the American Journal of Public Health focuses on parallels between marketing for cigarettes and UPFsThe new issue of the American Journal of Public Health focuses on ultra-processed foods, and reveals that big tobacco companies used strategies that helped them sell cigarettes to sell ultra-processed food products, including Lunchables, geared toward children.The parallels between ultra-processed foods (UPFs) and cigarettes include not only how UPF products were formulated and marketed to drive excess consumption, but also the growing body of evidence linking UPFs to a variety of health risks. For UPFs, these include cardiovascular diseases, certain cancers and cognitive health decline. Continue reading...

entry.title: Dem Mayor Karen Bass tops L.A. primary, faces possible general election showdown with Spencer Pratt
entry.content: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass moved a step closer to winning reelection after securing the top spot in the city's jungle primary -- putting her on a possible crash course this November with former bad-boy reality star Spencer Pratt.

entry.title: Chwalinska's remarkable French Open run continues as she beats Kalinskaya to reach semifinals
entry.content: Maja Chwalinska extended her remarkable French Open run by beating No. 22-seeded Anna Kalinskaya 7-6 (3), 6-3 Wednesday to reach the semifinals. The unseeded Chwalinska came through three qualifying rounds to become only the second Polish woman to reach the semifinals at Roland Garros, along with four-time champion Iga Swiatek. Kalinskaya's wild shot at the net gave Chwalinska a match point and she converted it when Kalinskaya's big forehand from the back of the court went out.

entry.title: Nottingham Forest prepare second bid for Ziolkowski
entry.content: Jan Ziolkowski is close to leaving Roma. The Polish defender, who arrived last summer from Legia Warsaw for just €6 million, is highly sought after by English club Nottingham Forest, a semi-finalist...

entry.title: Enrique Riquelme says he wants Barcelona in the Second Division – ‘It would make me happy’
entry.content: As the Real Madrid presidential election campaign enters its decisive phase, candidate Enrique Riquelme is continuing to outline the principles that would define his leadership if elected.Appearing on...

entry.title: Calder Cup playoffs schedule 2026: Bracket, times, TV channels, live streams, scores for AHL hockey postseason
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know to watch as a new Calder Cup champion is crowned in 2026.

entry.title: Texas A&M ranked Top 15 in The Athletic's preseason CFB rankings
entry.content: Texas A&M comes in at No. 13 in the Athletic's preseason Top 25, highlighting the offseason losses, and QB Marcel Reed's need to improve.

entry.title: Jonah Coleman discusses first NFL game, playing for Sean Payton
entry.content: Jonah Coleman spoke with NFL Network about playing for Broncos coach Sean Payton (and more) in a recent interview.

entry.title: Where to watch the World Cup at the stadium, with a crowd, or online
entry.content: Watching the 2026 FIFA World Cup will take many different forms for Washington soccer fans this summer, including online streams and in-person events.

entry.title: Kidnappings, threats and ‘protection fees’: how can Mexico confront rise in deadly extortion?
entry.content: With corrupt police on the streets and shopkeepers forced to pay gangs, president has vowed to tackle crime that now affects all parts of societyIt was about 11pm and Luis* was about to get into an Uber to go home when the police car pulled up. One of the officers produced two plastic bags with what looked like drugs: one contained some sort of powder, the other little crystals. Luis had never seen them before.Luis, who asked not to use his real name for fear of reprisals, insisted that the drugs weren’t his, but the officers didn’t seem to care. They shoved him into the back of the police truck and drove into the night. Continue reading...

entry.title: President Trump seeks control of science funding
entry.content: The White House Office of Management and Budget is moving to take more control of billions of dollars in federal grants. Critics say the proposed change would jeopardize the integrity of U.S. science.

entry.title: Meta took down over a million scam accounts in joint operation with Microsoft, SpaceX and DOJ
entry.content: Meta, Microsoft, SpaceX, Coinbase, the DOJ and international law enforcement partners collaborated in an operation that focused on scam networks in SE Asia.

entry.title: Federal workers experiencing ‘PTSD-like symptoms’ after unlawful firings by Trump administration
entry.content: In survey of more than 300 fired probationary employees, 95% reported continuing mental health effectsUS federal workers laid off by the Trump administration said they are experiencing mental health effects, including PTSD-like symptoms, from losing their jobs, according to a new survey.More than 300 fired probationary employees were surveyed, with 95% reporting ongoing mental health effects, according to 27UNIHTED, a network of former National Institute of Health (NIH) employees. Nearly half said they are experiencing PTSD-like symptoms, and a quarter are taking new medications to manage symptoms. Continue reading...

entry.title: Researchers show how AI-powered worms could wreak havoc on the internet
entry.content: Researchers created an AI-powered worm that that can spread through networks with no human intervention.

entry.title: The doctor who mends broken brains: why there is room for hope after a stroke or head injury
entry.content: The neurologist Orlando Swayne doesn’t suggest everyone can recover. But he does argue that early, targeted and intense therapy can sometimes bring about life-changing improvements – and we have a moral obligation to provide itClaire was in bad shape. She had been brought to the ward on a stretcher and hoisted on to a bed where she lay curled up in a ball. She was unable to speak, her eyes flat and face expressionless. While she could move her right arm a little, her left arm and both legs were immobile.Life had changed dramatically for Claire, a mother of three in her late 30s, many months earlier, when she collapsed while on a night out with friends. A weakness in an artery at the base of her brain had ruptured, spilling blood around her frontal lobe. She was taken to hospital, where surgeons removed two side plate-sized pieces of bone from her skull to relieve the pressure on her brain. She spent months in intensive care. Continue reading...

entry.title: 'Our bus was going up in smoke': Jos Buttler reveals scary incident after Gujarat Titans' IPL 2026 final loss to RCB
entry.content: Gujarat Titans' Jos Buttler described a frightening safety scare after their IPL final loss. The team bus caught fire due to a short circuit, forcing players and staff to evacuate. This incident compounded their disappointment after falling short against Royal Challengers Bengaluru, adding a bizarre end to their campaign.

entry.title: ‘They haven’t got the message’ – Florentino Perez fires back at Real Madrid sale accusations from Enrique Riquelme
entry.content: Florentino Perez has publicly responded to one of the biggest talking points of the ongoing Real Madrid presidential election campaign, rejecting claims that he is preparing the club for a future sale...

entry.title: ☕️🥐 FC Breakfast: the transfer window heated up overnight!
entry.content: Transfer update: things moved overnight 🔥According to Fabrizio Romano, Denzel Dumfries is expected to join Real Madrid. Another departure in Italy, with Ederson signing for Manchester United for �...

entry.content: Real Madrid and Atlético Madrid Eye Alexis Mac Allister TransferMadrid Interest Places Liverpool on AlertAlexis Mac Allister has become one of those players whose value is measured not only in assist...

entry.content: And if wishes were fishes…

entry.content: Morgan Rogers Transfer Interest: Aston Villa Face Arsenal Test After Breakout SeasonMorgan Rogers has become the sort of footballer who changes the temperature of a summer. Not because Aston Villa wan...

entry.title: Bayern Munich reach agreement with Ismaël Saibari
entry.content: Bayern Munich have reached an agreement with PSV Eindhoven attacker Ismaël Saibari (25), according to a report by Foot Mercato.Saibari, who scored 15 goals and provided eight assists in 27 Eredivisie...

entry.title: Bears have no choice but to make this trade after Myles Garrett deal with Rams
entry.content: The Bears have to keep up.

entry.title: 🚨 €45m deal! Manchester United seal summer transfer
entry.content: This transfer was a tough piece of work for Manchester United. But now the Red Devils have agreed a deal with Atalanta Bergamo and Éderson. That is being reported by both 'The Athletic', as well as t...

entry.title: NBA Insider Reveals ‘Strong’ Connection Between Knicks and Giannis Antetokounmpo And How It Will Play Out Come Next Season
entry.content: The New York Knicks were among the most heavily-linked to Giannis Antetokounmpo throughout the season. Neither the Greek Freak nor his current team the Milwaukee Bucks confirmed it but rumors lived on. Shams Charania is among the reporters relentless in reporting about Antetokounmpo’s move away from Wisconsin and in his latest scoop, he revealed that [...]

entry.title: One factor not expected to be considered in Vikings' decision to start J.J. McCarthy and Kyler Murray
entry.content: What could decide who starts?

entry.title: Celtics now viewed as free agent suitor for only player who has played well against Knicks in NBA Playoffs
entry.content: Could Boston land this FA?

entry.title: Chelsea set sights on Serie A star who Xabi Alonso wanted at Real Madrid
entry.content: Chelsea are reportedly interested in signing Juventus left-back Andrea Cambiaso.The Blues endured a challenging 2025-26 campaign, with both Enzo Maresca and Liam Rosenior losing their jobs after poor ...
